description_long: This is a rustic, cylindrical kettle with a slightly bulbous body tapering towards a flat, circular base. The surface appears to be made of aged copper or brass, showing a warm brownish patina with some darker spots, suggesting frequent use or exposure to heat. A long, straight spout projects horizontally from the upper side, while a tall, slender handle or stirring stick extends vertically from the center of the lid. The lid is flat and fits snugly on top, with the handle or stick emerging from its center, likely for stirring or lifting. The base is slightly wider than the body, providing stability, and the overall design is utilitarian with no visible decorative patterns. The object exudes a handcrafted, traditional aesthetic, suitable for boiling or pouring liquids in an urban or outdoor setting.
description: A rustic, cylindrical kettle with a long spout, flat lid, and tall central handle, made of aged metal with a plain, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The kettle is seen from an angle showing the long spout extending to the right and the tall handle or stick rising from the center of the flat lid.
description_view_1: The spout is viewed head-on, appearing as a straight projection from the body, with the handle or stick directly above the lid.
description_view_2: The spout extends to the left, and the body’s cylindrical shape and patina are clearly visible, with the base slightly flared.
description_view_3: The back of the kettle is visible, showing the plain, rounded body and the tall handle or stick, with no spout or handle visible from this angle.
attribute_lid_design: flat removable lid with central stick
